evidence: Every handler/utility class in api/ is package-private (ConcordanceHandlers, CorpusQueryHandlers, ExplorationHandlers, ExploreResponseAssembler, HttpApiUtils, RequestEntityTooLargeException, SketchHandlers, VisualizationHandlers), but RadialPlot is 'public class RadialPlot' in the same package — the sole public non-server class in api/.
RadialPlot is public apparently to allow RadialPlotTest to live in a different test package (viz/), creating an asymmetry: all api/ internals are hidden except this one visualization utility.

In model/exploration/, nearly all domain types are records (AdjectiveProfile, CoreCollocate, DiscoveredNoun, Edge, ExplorationOptions, SingleSeedExplorationOptions), but ComparisonResult is a plain class with no Javadoc explaining the deviation — unlike ExplorationResult which has a detailed 'Why a class rather than a record' block.

GrammarConfig.java uses fully-qualified inline annotations (@org.jspecify.annotations.NonNull, @org.jspecify.annotations.Nullable) on two methods while the same annotations are imported at the top of RelationConfig.java and all other files that use jspecify — a minor but visible style island within the config package.
package-info.java is present in every package (api, config, exploration, indexer/blacklab, model, model/exploration, query, utils) — a uniformly applied positive convention.

strengths: Naming conventions are uniformly Java camelCase throughout — no snake_case drift, no mixed identifier styles across packages.

Visibility conventions are consistent within each package: all api/ handler classes are package-private, all config/ and model/ public API types are public, and exploration/ internal collaborators (CollocateProfileComparator, MultiSeedExplorer) are package-private.
package-info.java is present in every production package without exception — an unusually consistent organizational discipline.
Logger initialization follows the same pattern (LoggerFactory.getLogger(ClassName.class)) in every class that logs, with no deviation.
Record vs. class choice for model value objects is mostly principled and self-documenting (ExplorationResult has an explicit 'Why a class' Javadoc block).
convention_outlier.issue_character: The issues are isolated style islands — one public class in a package-private-dominant handler package, one undocumented record-vs-class deviation, and two fully-qualified annotations in an import-dominant codebase — rather than a systemic naming or organization breakdown.
convention_outlier.score_rationale: The codebase is solidly consistent on the dimensions that matter most: naming, package structure, visibility, and behavioral protocols are uniform across sibling modules. The outliers — RadialPlot's public visibility in an otherwise package-private api/ package, and ComparisonResult's undocumented deviation from the record-dominant model/exploration/ convention — are real but minor; a developer would notice them as 'why is this different?' moments without being significantly impeded. This places the codebase above 80 (solid but uneven) and below 90 (only minor friction), landing at 83 where a small number of concrete, identifiable outliers keep it from the stronger range.

issues.summary: RadialPlot is public in an otherwise uniformly package-private api/ handler package
evidence: Every implementation class in api/ (ConcordanceHandlers, CorpusQueryHandlers, ExplorationHandlers, ExploreResponseAssembler, HttpApiUtils, RequestEntityTooLargeException, SketchHandlers, VisualizationHandlers) is package-private; RadialPlot is the only public non-entry-point class in the package.

RadialPlotTest lives in test package pl.marcinmilkowski.word_sketch.viz rather than pl.marcinmilkowski.word_sketch.api, which is why RadialPlot must be public — but this forces an inconsistency on all production api/ code.
issues.suggestion: Move RadialPlotTest into the api test package (pl.marcinmilkowski.word_sketch.api) so RadialPlot can become package-private, matching every other handler-layer class. Alternatively, move RadialPlot to a dedicated visualization utility package (e.g. pl.marcinmilkowski.word_sketch.viz or utils) where being public is consistent with its sibling public utilities.

issues.summary: ComparisonResult is a plain class without rationale while all sibling exploration models are records
evidence: In model/exploration/, AdjectiveProfile, CoreCollocate, DiscoveredNoun, Edge, ExplorationOptions, and SingleSeedExplorationOptions are all records; ComparisonResult is a plain class with a conventional constructor and accessors.

ExplorationResult.java contains an explicit 'Why a class rather than a record' Javadoc block explaining the computed-accessor rationale; ComparisonResult has no such explanation despite having the same pattern (computed views like fullyShared(), partiallyShared(), specificTo()).

ComparisonResult's computed methods (fullyShared(), partiallyShared(), etc.) are present on AdjectiveProfile too, which is a record — weakening any implicit justification for ComparisonResult being a class.
issues.suggestion: Either convert ComparisonResult to a record (with the computed-view methods retained as instance methods, which records support) to match the dominant pattern, or add a 'Why a class rather than a record' Javadoc block explaining the rationale, matching the ExplorationResult precedent.

root_causes: RadialPlot's public visibility was likely introduced to support a test in a different package without moving the test file — a pragmatic shortcut that created a visibility outlier.
ComparisonResult predates or was written alongside ExplorationResult without following through on the documentation convention established there.
